_about: Per-field statute sidecar. This is an AI-PROPOSED cross-reference between form fields and Maine statute, NOT legal advice and NOT an official citation. Every per-field entry carries verified=false and MUST be confirmed against the current Maine Revised Statutes before it is relied on. Section numbers below are candidates to check, not assertions of law. The form-level governing_statute is taken verbatim from form.yaml (sourced from the Secretary of State header) and is the only entry treated as sourced.

verification.instruction: Open the cited title, confirm each proposed section still exists and says what the rationale claims, then set verified=true and record who verified it.

entity.home_jurisdiction_name.rationale: Identifies the foreign entity whose home office address is changing; foreign authority to transact business is governed by 13-C Chapter 15, but no specific section is asserted here.

entity.home_office_address_new.physical.rationale: The form forbids a P.O. box for the physical home office line. The no-P.O.-box rule is enforced by the rubric check 'new-physical-not-po-box'; the statutory basis (if any) for the physical-address requirement is unconfirmed.

entity.maine_authorization_date.rationale: Date the foreign entity was authorized to transact business in Maine; tied to the certificate of authority provisions of 13-C Chapter 15, exact section unconfirmed.

filing.authorized_by.rationale: Per the form's footnote, this notification is filed by a Town/Municipality or the U.S. Postal Service (an E-911 address change), not by the entity itself. The statutory hook for a municipality/USPS-initiated home-office address correction is unconfirmed and should be checked before citing.
